SUMMARY 
Applied AI Engineer with hands-on experience building and deploying Python-based ML and LLM systems used in 
real-world workflows. Strong background across the full AI lifecycle, including data pipeline design, model training and 
evaluation, API-backed services, and deployment-ready ML systems. Experienced with Retrieval-Augmented

evaluation, API-backed services, and deployment-ready ML systems. Experienced with Retrieval-Augmented 
Generation, agentic LLM workflows, and cloud-based experimentation. Comfortable working directly with stakeholders 
and taking ownership of end-to-end AI solutions from problem definition to customer success. 
 
EDUCATION 
MSc Advanced Computer Science with AI | University of Strathclyde, UK | Sep 2024 – Sep 2025

and taking ownership of end-to-end AI solutions from problem definition to customer success.

MSc Advanced Computer Science with AI | University of Strathclyde, UK | Sep 2024 – Sep 2025 
 Modules: Machine Learning, Deep Learning, Business Analysis, Big Data Tools and Techniques, 
Quantitative Methods for Artificial Intelligence. 
Dissertation: Experimental evaluation of NLP and Large Language Model systems, with emphasis on

Quantitative Methods for Artificial Intelligence. 
Dissertation: Experimental evaluation of NLP and Large Language Model systems, with emphasis on 
bias detection, robustness analysis, and retrieval-augmented generation. 
 
WORK EXPERIENCE 
Research Intern | NeuraSearch Laboratory | Glasgow, UK | Oct 2024 – Dec 2024 
 Developed and evaluated AI prototypes for Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems, 
supporting applied experimentation and internal tooling.

 Developed and evaluated AI prototypes for Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems, 
supporting applied experimentation and internal tooling. 
 Built reproducible data ingestion, processing, and analytics pipelines to support scalable AI 
experimentation. 
 Monitored model behaviour and performance metrics, supporting iterative improvements and system 
refinement. 
 Translated technical findings into clear insights for both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

refinement. 
 Translated technical findings into clear insights for both technical and non-technical stakeholders. 
AI Engineer | CL Techno | London, UK | Feb 2024 – Feb 2025 
 Built applied AI prototypes for face recognition and emotion classification using Python and deep 
learning frameworks. 
 Integrated ML models into simple user-facing applications to support real-time inference and 
visualisation. 
 Evaluated model performance and refined pipelines based on empirical testing and feedback.

visualisation. 
 Evaluated model performance and refined pipelines based on empirical testing and feedback. 
 Collaborated on deploying and maintaining containerised AI services using Docker. 
 
PROJECTS 
 RAG Bias Auditor  
 Developed an end-to-end Generative AI pipeline for auditing bias in Retrieval-Augmented 
Generation systems 
 Implemented automated workflows for document ingestion, embedding generation, vector search, 
and bias scoring.

Generation systems 
 Implemented automated workflows for document ingestion, embedding generation, vector search, 
and bias scoring. 
 Analysed large-scale retrieval and generation outputs to identify systematic bias and inefficiencies. 
 Presented results using Jupyter-based visualisations to support transparent analysis and system 
optimisation. 
 Applied responsible AI principles to support transparent, explainable, and auditable AI decision-
making. 
 
TECHNICAL SKILLS

optimisation. 
 Applied responsible AI principles to support transparent, explainable, and auditable AI decision-
making. 
 
TECHNICAL SKILLS 
Languages & Tools: Python, MySQL, Pandas, Numpy, Spark 
ML & AI Frameworks: PyTorch, Tensorflow, Scikit-learn, CNN, Hugging Face Transformers, statistical 
analysis, feature engineering 
Generative AI & LLMs: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), LangChain, embeddings, prompt pipelines, 
bias evaluation

analysis, feature engineering 
Generative AI & LLMs: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), LangChain, embeddings, prompt pipelines, 
bias evaluation 
MLOps & Deployment: Docker, AWS (EC2, S3), model evaluation, experiment tracking, reproducible 
pipelines 
Data & Search Systems: FAISS, vector databases, large-scale document retrieval

Development & DevOps: Git/GitHub 
APIs & Integration: REST APIs (model inference & data integration) 
Collaboration & Communication: Technical documentation, stakeholder communication, research reporting 
Production AI Concepts: Model evaluation, latency awareness, basic monitoring, cost-conscious 
experimentation

The place of useful learning 
The University of Strathclyde is a charitable body, registered in Scotland, number SC015263 
Participant Information Sheet 
Name of department: Computer and Information Sciences 
Title of the study: Touch study using simulated Parkinson's Tremor 
Thank you for your interest in this study. The study is part of on-going research into mobile support for 
people with Parkinson's Tremor. The study is being conducted by Dr Mark Dunlop, a senior lecturer in the

people with Parkinson's Tremor. The study is being conducted by Dr Mark Dunlop, a senior lecturer in the 
Digital Health and Wellness Research Group at Strathclyde. The study will involve you using two pieces of 
equipment: 
• A standard smartphone with software to measure tap accuracy and phone movement;
• A Parkinson's Tremor Simulation kit that is based around a TENS
(transcutaneous electrical nerve stimulation) unit. TENS units are off-the-shelf,

• A Parkinson's Tremor Simulation kit that is based around a TENS
(transcutaneous electrical nerve stimulation) unit. TENS units are off-the-shelf,
drug-free, non-invasive units that use a low-voltage electrical current to
stimulate nerves and muscles in the body. They are typically used for pain-relief
or muscle massage. More information on TENS is available from the NHS at
www.nhs.uk/conditions/transcutaneous-electrical-nerve-stimulation-tens .
What is the purpose of this research?

or muscle massage. More information on TENS is available from the NHS at
www.nhs.uk/conditions/transcutaneous-electrical-nerve-stimulation-tens .
What is the purpose of this research? 
To collect data on mobile phone touch screen activity from people in normal use and people wearing a TENS 
powered Parkinson's simulation glove. This will help us understand how the tremor affects touch and we 
hope that the results will form the basis for academic publication and future research. 
Do you have to take part?

hope that the results will form the basis for academic publication and future research. 
Do you have to take part? 
No. Participation is voluntary and you are free to stop at any point. 
What will you do in the project? 
You are asked to attend a single meeting in Mark Dunlop's office (L7.32) Livingstone Tower. In this meeting 
you will be asked to complete a short demographic form, get a demonstration of the TENS system then use

you will be asked to complete a short demographic form, get a demonstration of the TENS system then use 
the mobile app to tap on circles on the screen in four short tests with different TENS settings. Each test will 
last about 3 minutes followed by a short questionnaire and a little rest before continuing. You will be asked to 
wear a Parkinson's simulator glove on your non-dominant hand in which you will also hold a phone, wear a

wear a Parkinson's simulator glove on your non-dominant hand in which you will also hold a phone, wear a 
TENS electrode on the bicep of the same arm, and tap the phone with a finger on your dominant hand. The 
whole study will take no more than 1 hour. 
For taking part we will give you a small gift token of our appreciation and enter you into a draw for a larger 
token. Asking to stop will not affect these tokens. 
Continued…. 
NHS TENS Information

2 
Why have you been invited to take part?  
Adverts have been widely circulated and we are looking for a wide range of participants. While TENS units 
are widely used and very safe for most people, it is important to pay attention to the exclusion criteria below. 
TENS units must not be used by people in the following categories except under medical advice - if you are 
in one of these categories you must not take part in this study: 
1. People who are under 18;

If you feel that you match one of these conditions then please let Mark know - you do not need to say 
what the condition is, just that you match one of the exclusion criteria and cannot continue. 
During the study you will be asked to wear a glove. For the hygiene of all participants, you will be asked to 
use a disinfectant hand gel before and after use. You may bring your own if you have a preferred brand. 
What information is being collected in the project?

use a disinfectant hand gel before and after use. You may bring your own if you have a preferred brand. 
What information is being collected in the project?  
We will record your tap locations on the study mobile phone along with data from sensors in the mobile 
phone. This will be linked with your demographic information and completed forms. No identifiable data will 
be recorded by the phone and all information will be held anonymously.

phone. This will be linked with your demographic information and completed forms. No identifiable data will 
be recorded by the phone and all information will be held anonymously. 
If you are comfortable and permit, Mark may take a photograph of you using the equipment after the study. 
This may be used in publications but, again, anonymously. 
Who will have access to the information? 
This work is conducted together with Prof Scott Mackenzie of York University, Canada. Anonymous data

Who will have access to the information? 
This work is conducted together with Prof Scott Mackenzie of York University, Canada. Anonymous data 
from the mobile phone and your completed forms will be shared with Scott. Summary data will be used in 
publications and future grant applications and the full data set may be made public through open-access data 
initiatives - all anonymously. 
Continued…

3 
Where will the information be stored and how long will it be kept for? 
Data will be held on secure Strathclyde servers (shared with York Uni). Anonymous data will be held long 
term and potentially made open access. If you wish your data to be removed, please contact Mark within 90 
days of the study - after this the record of meeting times will be deleted and the data will no longer be linked 
with your appointment and identity so cannot be deleted. Consent forms will be kept secure for 10 years.

with your appointment and identity so cannot be deleted. Consent forms will be kept secure for 10 years. 
Thank you for reading this information – please ask any questions if you are unsure about what is written 
here. Please read our Privacy Notice for Research Participants for more information about your legal rights.  
What happens next? 
If you are comfortable to continue or have any further questions, please let Mark know. If you do not want to

What happens next? 
If you are comfortable to continue or have any further questions, please let Mark know. If you do not want to 
be involved - then thank you for your time and consideration.  No feedback will be given after the study, but 
you will be asked if you wish to receive a copy of any publication directly resulting from it. 
More Information & Contact Details 
Parkinson’s is a progressive condition that affects about 1 in 100 over 60s in the UK. If you have concerns

More Information & Contact Details 
Parkinson’s is a progressive condition that affects about 1 in 100 over 60s in the UK. If you have concerns 
about Parkinson’s or need support, Parkinson’s UK have detailed information at www.parkinsons.org.uk and 
provide a support phone line at 0808 800 0303 as well as being on Facebook, X and Instagram.  
Research Contact:  
Dr Mark Dunlop, Computer & Information Sciences, Strathclyde University, Glasgow G1 1XH.  
Email: mark.dunlop@strath.ac.uk
